infobox.value: at the **[1999 Pan American Games](/wiki/1999_Pan_American_Games "1999 Pan American Games")** was held at [University Stadium](/wiki/University_Stadium_(Winnipeg) "University Stadium (Winnipeg)") in [Winnipeg, Manitoba](/wiki/Winnipeg "Winnipeg"), Canada. Two new events were introduced for women: pole vault and hammer throw. In addition the 20 km road walk replaced the 10,000 m track walk.

The **[athletics competition](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)")** at the **[1999 Pan American Games](/wiki/1999_Pan_American_Games "1999 Pan American Games")** was held at [University Stadium](/wiki/University_Stadium_(Winnipeg) "University Stadium (Winnipeg)") in [Winnipeg, Manitoba](/wiki/Winnipeg "Winnipeg"), Canada. Two new events were introduced for women: pole vault and hammer throw. In addition the 20 km road walk replaced the 10,000 m track walk.

## Medal table

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Athletics_at_the_1999_Pan_American_Games&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Medal table")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/46/Bernard_Williams.jpg/250px-Bernard_Williams.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bernard_Williams.jpg)

[Bernard Williams](/wiki/Bernard_Williams_(athlete) "Bernard Williams (athlete)") won the men's 100 m gold for the United States.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/34/Osaka07_D2M_400m_Ana_Guevara_Novlene_Williams.jpg/250px-Osaka07_D2M_400m_Ana_Guevara_Novlene_Williams.jpg)](/wiki/File:Osaka07_D2M_400m_Ana_Guevara_Novlene_Williams.jpg)

In 1999, [Ana Guevara](/wiki/Ana_Guevara "Ana Guevara") of Mexico won the first of her three 400 m titles.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/63/Maurren_Maggi2.jpg/250px-Maurren_Maggi2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Maurren_Maggi2.jpg)

Brazil's [Maurren Maggi](/wiki/Maurren_Maggi "Maurren Maggi") took long jump gold and 100 m hurdles silver.
